articles.title: IMD issues yellow alert in Mumbai, Thane and Palghar as it braces for heat and storms - Mid-day
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Maharashtra
articles.full_text:
IMD issues yellow alert in Mumbai, Thane and Palghar as it braces for heat and storms
IMD has issued a yellow alert for Mumbai and several parts of Maharashtra, including Thane, Palghar, Konkan, Marathwada, and Vidarbha on Thursday. The state is expected to see a mix of heatwave conditions, humidity, and thunderstorms over the next five days, with isolated rain, lightning and gusty winds in several regions
Maharashtra to see heat, humidity and thunderstorms this week. Representational Image
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow alert for several parts of Maharashtra on Thursday, including Mumbai, Thane and Palghar. The alert also covers coastal and southern districts stretching up to Kolhapur, along with parts of Marathwada such as Nanded, Latur and Solapur.
The warning comes as the state prepares for a mix of hot weather, humidity and thunderstorm activity over the next five days.
